title: Visual Ingestion
summary: Simulate passing non-digital materials into the system, such as handwritten notes or short audio clips, and show the extracted transcription on the right after upload.
uploadNote: This upload is only for the visual design. Select either a handwritten note sample or a short lecture audio sample, then press the button to simulate transcription.
examplesCaption: Choose either the handwriting sample or the audio sample below to see a different transcription output.
outputCaption: The right panel shows a sample transcription for the currently selected handwriting or audio upload.

exampleInputs.label: Handwritten lecture notes
exampleInputs.outputText:
Preliminary linear algebra notes

- A vector is an ordered list of numbers.
- We begin with vectors in R^2 and R^3.
- Vector addition happens component by component.
- Scalar multiplication changes magnitude and may reverse direction.
- A matrix can be used to represent a linear transformation.

exampleInputs.label: Lecture audio clip
exampleInputs.outputText:
Preliminary linear algebra lecture audio

- Today we are building intuition before moving into formal proofs.
- You can think of a vector as an arrow with both magnitude and direction.
- Matrix-vector multiplication tells us how a transformation acts on that arrow.
- Next class we will connect these ideas to systems of linear equations and span.
